evals.prompt: I have a BRIEFING.md at ~/.claude/BRIEFING.md with authors (Boris Cherny), topics (Claude Code), and sites (claude.com/blog). It has three analyses: 'Industry trends that are likely to last', 'Concrete suggestions for how workflow should change to leverage Agentic Engineering', and 'Inspiring usages of Agentic AI'. Run a briefing to discover fresh content on these topics.
evals.expected_output: Updated BRIEFING_SECTIONS.md with timestamped entry containing 3-5 items per analysis in Slack-ready format. Updated BRIEFING_LINKS.md with new URLs appended. Summary showing items found per analysis, deduplicated count, filtered count.

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a new briefing for the first time. I have BRIEFING.md in ~/.claude/ but no BRIEFING_LINKS.md or BRIEFING_SECTIONS.md yet. The briefing tracks 'agentic-engineering' topic and 'Dario Amodei' author. Run the briefing to initialize and populate both tracking files.
evals.expected_output: Both BRIEFING_LINKS.md and BRIEFING_SECTIONS.md created from scratch. BRIEFING_SECTIONS.md has first timestamped entry. BRIEFING_LINKS.md contains all discovered URLs. Summary confirms files were initialized.

evals.prompt: I have a project-specific briefing at /Users/ssanders/Projects/ml-infra/BRIEFING.md. Run the briefing using that config file instead of the default. It tracks 'ML infrastructure' and 'distributed systems' topics.
evals.expected_output: Updated /Users/ssanders/Projects/ml-infra/BRIEFING_SECTIONS.md and /Users/ssanders/Projects/ml-infra/BRIEFING_LINKS.md (not the default ~/.claude/ ones). Summary confirms correct paths were used.
